subject: English
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – Weathering the Storm in Ersama
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: On 27 October 1999, Prashant had gone to Ersama, a small town in Orissa, to spend the day with a friend. Suddenly, a super cyclone hit the region. Winds began to blow at a speed of nearly 350 kilometres per hour. The sea rose and heavy rain started pouring. Within hours, trees were uprooted and houses were destroyed.
questions: Where had Prashant gone on 27 October 1999?
What natural disaster struck the region?
How fast were the cyclone winds blowing?
What happened to the trees and houses?
What natural forces caused destruction?

passages.paragraph: The storm continued for more than thirty-six hours. Floodwaters rose rapidly and surrounded the house where Prashant had taken shelter. To escape the rising water, Prashant and his friend’s family climbed onto the roof of the house. They stayed there for two days without proper food or drinking water.
questions: How long did the storm continue?
What surrounded the house during the cyclone?
Where did Prashant and the family take shelter?
How long did they remain there?
What difficulties did they face on the roof?

passages.paragraph: Fortunately, some coconut trees had fallen on the roof of the house. These coconuts became the only source of food for the trapped family. They broke the coconuts and drank the water inside them. In this way, they managed to survive the difficult days of the cyclone.
questions: What fell onto the roof of the house?
How did the coconuts help the family?
What did the family drink from the coconuts?
Why were the coconuts important for survival?
How did the family manage to survive?

passages.paragraph: After the rain stopped, Prashant decided to return to his village, which was about eighteen kilometres away. The roads were flooded, and he had to walk through deep water. Sometimes he had to swim. He carried a long stick to find the road and to check the depth of the water.
questions: Why did Prashant decide to return to his village?
How far was his village from Ersama?
What difficulties did he face during the journey?
Why did he carry a long stick?
What did the stick help him do?

passages.paragraph: As Prashant travelled through the flooded areas, he saw terrible scenes of destruction. Dead bodies of people and animals were floating in the water. Houses had collapsed and villages had been completely destroyed. The sight filled him with shock and sadness.
questions: What did Prashant see in the floodwaters?
What had happened to the houses?
How were the villages affected?
How did these sights affect Prashant emotionally?
What kind of destruction did the cyclone cause?

passages.paragraph: When Prashant finally reached his village, he found that his own house had been destroyed. Many villagers had taken shelter in a Red Cross building. There were about 2500 people crowded into the shelter. Most of them had lost their homes and loved ones.
questions: What happened to Prashant’s house?
Where had the villagers taken shelter?
Approximately how many people were in the shelter?
Why were people staying there?
What losses had the villagers suffered?

passages.paragraph: There was very little food in the shelter, and people were starving. Prashant organised a group of young volunteers to help the villagers. Together they persuaded a merchant to give them rice for cooking. They used branches of fallen trees to light fires and prepare food.
questions: What major problem did the villagers face in the shelter?
What did Prashant organise to help the villagers?
How did they get rice for food?
What did they use to make fire?
How did Prashant help solve the food problem?

passages.paragraph: After a few days, a military helicopter began dropping food parcels for the survivors. To attract the helicopter’s attention, children lay on the ground with empty utensils on their stomachs. This unusual signal showed that they were hungry and needed food urgently.
questions: Who dropped food parcels for the villagers?
What did the children do to signal hunger?
Why did they place utensils on their stomachs?
What message were they trying to send?
How did the helicopter help the survivors?

passages.paragraph: Many children had lost their parents during the cyclone and were left orphaned. Prashant arranged for these children to stay together in the shelter. Women from the village volunteered to look after them and provide care and comfort.
questions: Why were many children orphaned?
What did Prashant do for the orphaned children?
Who took responsibility for caring for them?
Where were the children kept?
Why was community care important?

passages.paragraph: Prashant also organised sports activities such as cricket matches to help children overcome their sadness. He encouraged women to join the food-for-work programme so they could rebuild their lives. Through courage, cooperation, and leadership, the villagers slowly recovered from the disaster.
questions: What activities did Prashant organise for children?
Why were sports activities important?
What programme helped women rebuild their lives?
Which qualities helped the villagers recover?
What message does the story convey about disasters?
